Scenario.title: ShadowRay Campaign Targets Transportation Systems

overview.description: The ShadowRay campaign exploits vulnerabilities in transportation systems to disrupt operations, leveraging basic-level attacks to achieve their goals.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disruption of critical infrastructure and potential financial gain.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 180 Name: Exploiting Incorrectly Configured Access Control Security Levels
stage.description: The attackers exploit a known vulnerability in the transportation system's software to gain initial access. This involves targeting improperly configured access controls, allowing unauthorized entry into the system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 88 Name: OS Command Injection
stage.description: Once initial access is achieved, the attackers inject malicious commands into the system to execute arbitrary code. This technique allows them to gain control over critical system functions, enabling further actions.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 440 Name: Hardware Integrity Attack
stage.description: The attackers deploy disruptive commands or malware to interfere with the normal operation of transportation systems. This results in operational disruptions, potentially leading to safety risks and financial losses.

Scenario.summary: The ShadowRay campaign successfully compromises transportation systems through a series of basic yet effective attacks. By exploiting vulnerabilities, executing malicious commands, and disrupting operations, the attackers achieve their objective of causing chaos and potential financial gain.
